ea_prompt.required_attachments: <attachment path="C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.github\copilot-instructions.md" reason="Post-EA-2 BlarAI XML doctrine. EA-3 reads to confirm `` element (L134-136) resolves to `` element EA-3 authors in devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md, and `` (L165) resolves to `` + `` envelopes EA-3 authors."/>
ea_prompt.working_directory_exception:
**CRITICAL — read this BEFORE the comprehension gate.**
EA Code's wake_launcher creates a worktree at `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI-worktrees\ea_code_` and injects a "do not cd elsewhere" directive into your session. EA-3 writes to a **separate repo** (`C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform`). The "do not cd elsewhere" directive does NOT apply to this EA.
Operate as follows:
1. **Verify devplatform repo state from the worktree** via: ```powershell git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" status --porcelain git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" log --oneline -3 main ```
2. **Use `git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" ...` for git operations**, OR cd to `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform` for the duration of EA-3 work and cd back to the worktree at completion. Either pattern is acceptable. The `git -C` pattern is preferred because it avoids accidental cwd drift.
3. **devplatform convention is direct-to-main commits** — NO feature branch. (SDV §5.1 #3 "commit on devplatform main per devplatform's existing direct-to-main convention (Stage 6.7.5 history establishes this pattern)".)
4. **Sprint reports + ledger entry commit to the BlarAI worktree**, not devplatform — per existing convention. The BlarAI worktree carries the sprint report + ledger entry + this EA's completion comment artifact; devplatform carries the four content artifacts (3 doctrine files + the SOP portability fix).
5. **NEVER push to either remote.** Local commits only. The LA merges via Co-Lead trusted_scope or la_merge_approve.ps1 (BlarAI side) or accepts direct-to-main (devplatform side) as already-landed.
Cross-repo file write summary: - **BlarAI worktree writes**: ledger entry + sprint completion report + (if used) trigger-file write for SDO wake. - **devplatform writes**: 3 doctrine files + SOP portability fix artifact(s). - **NO BlarAI feature branch** for EA-3 work — EA-3 has no BlarAI source-doctrine writes (EA-2 did all of those). - The BlarAI worktree's branch may be the same as EA-2's branch or main directly — the ledger + report are post-EA-2 metadata; commit them on main (Co-Lead will not need a merge — the LA reviews the completion report directly).
ea_prompt.pre_flight:
Sprint 10 EA-3 authors three devplatform doctrine files from scratch and resolves the `tools.autonomy_budget` import portability bug (SDV §4 success criterion #4). The working set is cross-repo: 3 devplatform doctrine files + at least 1 devplatform code artifact (the portability fix) + 1 BlarAI ledger entry + 1 BlarAI sprint completion report. EA-2 has already landed on BlarAI main (commit `1b1614e`); BlarAI's three doctrine files are post-strip with cross-reference pointers expecting EA-3's §sections to exist in devplatform.
**Substantive multi-file work across two repos** — fleet-hygiene §4 mandates pause. Use this workaround verbatim (SDV §9.2 #3 catalog: PYTHONPATH-prepend invocation is the EA-2-precedent pattern, and the very bug EA-3 will fix means EA-3 still uses the workaround for ITS OWN pause/resume invocations — the fix becomes effective AFTER EA-3 lands the resolved entry-point):
```powershell $env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Sprint 10 EA-3 devplatform doctrine authorship + SOP portability fix', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI ; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Sprint 10 EA-3 devplatform doctrine authorship" ```
Resume LAST, before exit (after EA-3's portability fix is committed; for symmetry with EA-2's workaround, the resume invocation uses the OLD pattern — testing the NEW pattern is part of EA-3's verification matrix, not its operational pause/resume flow):
```powershell $env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet(updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI ; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Sprint 10 EA-3 done" ```
**API-NAME GOTCHA** (L-21, repeated from EA-2): the function is `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet`. Calling `state.unpause_fleet(...)` raises `AttributeError`.
**Pause MUST land before any devplatform write.** Pause first; then verify devplatform repo clean state; then begin authoring.
ea_prompt.comprehension_gate:
BEFORE making any edits, running any `git checkout`, or invoking any pause/resume, post a comprehension comment to Vikunja task #369 prefixed `[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]` containing ALL of:
1. **Milestone objective in your own words.** What you are authoring, why, what the post-EA-3 devplatform doctrine surface looks like, and who reads devplatform's doctrine downstream (autonomous fleet sessions — SDO/Co-Lead/EA Code/Sprint Auditor running under wake_launcher; cf-1 EAs that will read these files cold without preloaded BlarAI context).
2. **Per-work-item summary.** WI-1 through WI-N (see §work_items below) — one sentence per WI in your own words.
3. **Files to create/modify (absolute paths).** Exactly the following files written: - `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md` (overwrite — placeholder stub replaced with full doctrine ≥ 100 lines) - `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\AGENTS.md` (overwrite — placeholder stub replaced with full dev/target framing ≥ 100 lines) - `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md` (overwrite — placeholder stub replaced with full XML doctrine ≥ 100 lines) - SOP portability fix artifact(s) under `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\` (chosen technique determines exact path — declare your choice in this gate) - `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\20260511_180000_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md` (new — Q1-1 ledger entry; UTC second-resolution stamp acceptable; if EA execution time differs materially adjust the stamp to actual EA-start UTC) - `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_10\reports\_ea_code_completion_v1.md` (new — sprint completion report; `` = UTC `YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS` captured at EA close)
4. **Source files to read.** The SDV; the classification matrix; the EA-2 prompt; the three post-EA-2 BlarAI doctrine files (to verify cross-reference resolution); the three devplatform placeholder stubs (to confirm overwrite, not append); `tools/autonomy_budget/state.py` (to choose portability fix technique); `tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml` (if config-level fix is chosen). No reads outside this list are required for EA-3's authoring.
5. **Classification matrix recitation — recite VERBATIM:** - MOVE-devplatform row count: **19** - MIRROR-both row count: **7** - Total content rows EA-3 authors fresh in devplatform: 19 (MOVE) + 7 (MIRROR, devplatform-side authoring) + 9 (matrix rows #45-53, devplatform-only fresh content with no BlarAI source) = **35 row-equivalents** distributed across the three devplatform files.
6. **EXACT deliverable structure — recite VERBATIM.** State: - No BlarAI feature branch for EA-3 work (no BlarAI source-doctrine writes) - devplatform commit pattern: direct to main, single commit per the commit_template below - BlarAI ledger entry filename: `docs/ledger/20260511_180000_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md` (stamp may be adjusted to actual EA-start UTC) - BlarAI sprint report filename pattern: `docs/sprints/sprint_10/reports/_ea_code_completion_v1.md` - Three devplatform doctrine files OVERWRITE the existing placeholder stubs (per matrix §4 F-2) - Each devplatform doctrine file ≥ 100 lines (SDV §4 #2, §5.3 mature-not-minimal floor)
7. **ORACLE expectation (L-12 ACTION-3) — recite VERBATIM:** ``` git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" diff 1a4713d..HEAD --name-only ``` must output exactly (sorted, modulo the portability fix files which depend on chosen technique): ``` .github/copilot-instructions.md AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md tools/autonomy_budget/ (e.g., cli.py if standalone-CLI chosen) ``` BlarAI-side ORACLE: ``` git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" diff 1b1614e..HEAD --name-only ``` must output exactly (sorted): ``` docs/ledger/20260511_180000_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md docs/sprints/sprint_10/reports/_ea_code_completion_v1.md ``` Any other path in either ORACLE → violation; STOP and report.
8. **Working-set declaration (L-15) — verbatim.** "I write to exactly six paths: three devplatform doctrine files, one (or more) devplatform portability-fix artifact, one BlarAI ledger entry, one BlarAI sprint completion report. I read the SDV, the classification matrix, the EA-2 prompt, the three post-EA-2 BlarAI doctrine files, the three devplatform placeholder stubs, and the autonomy_budget code. I touch NO other path in either repo. If I discover a defect outside the working set (e.g., a stale runbook reference in BlarAI's docs/runbooks/, a typo in an ADR), I STOP and document in my completion report; I do not freelance."
9. **Cross-repo ordering acknowledgment (L-19) — verbatim.** "EA-2 has already landed on BlarAI main at commit `1b1614e`. EA-3 commits to devplatform main second. My devplatform commit body explicitly cross-references the BlarAI EA-2 merge commit `1b1614e`. I do NOT push to either remote. I do NOT touch BlarAI doctrine files — they were EA-2's scope and are now frozen for the remainder of Sprint 10."
10. **Mature-not-minimal acknowledgment (L-22) — verbatim.** "Mature not minimal: each devplatform doctrine file is a coherent operational reference in its own right, readable standalone by a cf-1 EA or a Cowork sandbox agent that has never seen BlarAI runtime context. Floor: ≥ 100 lines per file. Target: substantive content, not padding. If a file approaches the floor only with placeholder bullet points or restated cross-references, I expand with concrete operational guidance from the matrix rows (MOVE-devplatform content) and from the SDV §5.3 (a)-(i) fleet-doctrine enumeration. Mature-not-minimal does NOT mean prolix — it means content density at the floor."
11. **LA arbitration acknowledgment.** Recite each of the five LA-arbitrated content directives one-line in your own words: (a) Row #41 — devplatform/AGENTS.md mirrors the dev/target framing from BlarAI/AGENTS.md, frames BlarAI as one of two target repos, ≥ 100 lines. (b) IR-9 — devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md OWNS the full `` element (BlarAI's `` at L134-136 expects it). (c) Row #37 — devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md OWNS `` + `` envelopes; cross-references BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions for the label-id table; does NOT duplicate numeric IDs. (d) Row #27 — devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md includes its own `` block (MIRROR-both — same LA framing, devplatform-context phrasing). (e) Row #12 — devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint authors the doctrine; describes how BlarAI's `docs/sprints/ACTIVE_SPRINT.md` is read; BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L91 `*See also:*` pointer resolves here.
12. **Cross-reference resolution audit acknowledgment.** Recite the EXACT BlarAI-side cross-reference pointers (post-EA-2) that EA-3's devplatform §sections must resolve: - `BlarAI/CLAUDE.md` L68: `*See also: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md §Vikunja-Bridge.*` - `BlarAI/CLAUDE.md` L91: `*See also: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint.*` - `BlarAI/CLAUDE.md` (post-Active-State): `*See also: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md §Agent-Operating-Model and §Fleet-Pause-SOP.*` - `BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md` L134-136 ``: points to `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop` - `BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md` L165 ``: points to `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md §vikunja_task_tracking` Confirm that devplatform/CLAUDE.md will contain §sections named: `Vikunja-Bridge`, `Current-Active-Sprint`, `Agent-Operating-Model`, `Fleet-Pause-SOP` — exact header text matters for grep-able cross-reference resolution. Confirm that devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md will contain XML elements named: `` (full SOP body), `` (containing `` + ``).
13. **Portability-fix technique choice — declare in this gate.** Choose ONE of the four SDV §9.2 #3 catalog options and state which: (a) Absolute `python -m tools.autonomy_budget.cli` invocation (requires sibling `cli.py` shipped in devplatform; doctrine commands use the -m form which `python` resolves via `sys.path[0]`/CWD or a PYTHONPATH-augmenting wrapper). (b) `sys.path` prepend in a wrapper script (`pause_fleet.py` or similar) shipped in devplatform with hardcoded `sys.path.insert(0, r"C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform")` before the import. (c) Standalone CLI script `tools/autonomy_budget/cli.py` invoked by absolute path (`python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py pause ""`) — the script itself handles the import path because Python's `sys.path[0]` becomes its containing directory. (d) PYTHONPATH-injecting `.cmd` or `.ps1` wrapper that sets `$env:PYTHONPATH` and shells out to python. State the choice and rationale (2 sentences max) in your comprehension comment. The matrix-driven default per SDV §9.2 #3 is **option (c)** as "likely (c) given Stage 6.7.5's PS1-script-with-env-var pattern". You may choose otherwise if the existing `state.py` shape favors a different option; document in the completion report.
14. **Risks and ambiguities.** State any unresolved understanding before starting. Examples: which fleet-doctrine sections from SDV §5.3 (a)-(i) you anticipate authoring with the most content density; whether you anticipate the XML envelope for `` needing more than the LA-described `` + `` envelopes (e.g., if you want to mirror BlarAI's `` envelope for fleet symmetry, declare so).
15. **Out-of-working-set prohibition acknowledgment (L-15 verbatim).** "DO NOT modify any file outside [3 devplatform doctrine files, devplatform portability-fix artifact(s), BlarAI ledger entry, BlarAI sprint completion report]. DO NOT touch BlarAI doctrine files (EA-2 scope, now frozen). DO NOT amend ADRs, governance docs, runbooks, test code, production code, services/, shared/, launcher/. DO NOT touch `docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md` (FROZEN). DO NOT create Stage 6.7.5 retro-fix tickets — those flow through SDO Stage 6.7.5 backlog separately. If a non-working-set change appears necessary, STOP, document the blocker in my completion report, and wait for SDO direction."
After posting the comprehension comment, apply label `Gate:Pending-SDO` (id 9) on Vikunja task #369 and STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1a review verdict before doing any work. Do NOT bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment.

milestone.objective: Author three devplatform doctrine files (`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`) from scratch (overwriting the existing 2-4 line placeholder stubs) using EA-1's classification matrix MOVE-devplatform + MIRROR-both rows and the LA-arbitrated content directives in Vikunja task #369 comment #521. Each file ≥ 100 lines, mature-not-minimal, self-readable by a cf-1 EA without preloaded BlarAI context. Resolve the `tools.autonomy_budget` import portability bug (SDV §4 success criterion #4) and verify pause + resume invocations succeed from 3 working directories. Land all four content artifacts on devplatform main via a single direct-to-main commit cross-referencing the BlarAI EA-2 merge commit `1b1614e`. Write a Q1-1 ledger entry and a sprint completion report on BlarAI main.
milestone.branch: direct-to-main (devplatform); ledger + report on BlarAI main directly (no feature branch)
milestone.size_rationale: Three doctrine files authored ≥ 100 lines each (≥ 300 lines combined floor; mature target ~400-600 lines combined) plus at least one Python file for the portability fix plus ledger + report. Combined adds will likely exceed trusted_scope 500-LOC threshold on the devplatform side, but devplatform uses direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5 convention — no merge-gate ESCALATE needed. The BlarAI-side ledger + report writes are small (~150-300 lines combined) and merge cleanly via Co-Lead trusted_scope OR direct to main per existing convention.

ea_prompt.la_arbitrated_directives:
The five content directives below come from LA arbitration on Vikunja task #369 comment #521 (2026-05-11 16:05 PDT). Implement VERBATIM where the LA supplies verbatim framing; encode the spirit faithfully where the LA supplies a directional call.
### Directive A — Row #41 — devplatform/AGENTS.md dev/target framing **Source**: LA comment #521 row #41 (LA-corrected). **Authorship rule**: devplatform/AGENTS.md is the dev-side entry for devplatform fleet infrastructure work. It mirrors the dev/target boundary framing established in BlarAI/AGENTS.md (which is a ~12-line pointer authored by EA-2). devplatform/AGENTS.md should: - State that devplatform is the dev-platform / fleet-infrastructure repo, NOT a runtime product. - State that Claude/Codex/Copilot are dev-side agents targeting two repos: BlarAI (product) and devplatform (this repo, fleet infrastructure). - Point at `devplatform/CLAUDE.md` for the fleet operating model authoritative reference. - Point at `devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md` for Copilot/dev-side-agent operating instructions. - List the fleet-coordination expectations for SDO / Co-Lead / EA-Code / Sprint Auditor agents — what their roles are in 1-2 sentences each. - Frame BlarAI as one of TWO target repos (the other being devplatform itself), explicitly named at `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI` with a pointer to its AGENTS.md. - State that BlarAI is a Qwen3-based locally-run product runtime; Claude is NEVER part of BlarAI's runtime (per LA's correction rationale). **Length floor**: ≥ 100 lines (SDV §5.3 mature-not-minimal). Mature target: 120-180 lines. **Tone**: dev-side operator-facing — assume the reader is a Codex/Cowork sandbox agent or a fleet-aware Copilot session that has never read BlarAI's docs.
### Directive B — IR-9 — devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md owns `` **Source**: LA comment #521 IR-9 (LA-corrected). **Authorship rule**: Fleet-pause SOP is a dev-side operational norm — NOT a BlarAI runtime constraint. devplatform's copilot-instructions.md authors the full `` element. BlarAI's copilot-instructions.md (post-EA-2) contains only a `` placeholder (verified live at L134-136 of post-EA-2 BlarAI file) cross-referencing devplatform's authoritative version.
The canonical SOP block (which EA-3 authors) MUST contain: - **Trigger conditions** — multi-commit dev sessions, branch-checkout work, merge operations, stash operations. (Per feedback memory `feedback_pause_fleet_for_git_work`.) - **The pause command** with the import portability fix already applied — i.e., the example command uses EA-3's chosen technique from §work_items WI-4, NOT the legacy `$env:PYTHONPATH` workaround. The SOP is the doctrine; the doctrine reflects the post-fix state. - **The resume command** — symmetric form with `resume_fleet` (NOT `unpause_fleet` — API-name gotcha noted explicitly in the SOP). - **The verification step** — how to confirm fleet is paused (`Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\state.json | ConvertFrom-Json | Select-Object paused, paused_by, paused_at, paused_reason`). - **The LA-coordination note** from feedback memory `feedback_pause_fleet_for_git_work` — fleet-pause is LA-coordinated across sessions; ambiguous instructions → read back and ask. Per feedback memory `feedback_monitor_fleet_state_between_turns`: "monitor" = observe + report; never auto-repause/unpause. - **The trivial-edit exception** — single-commit edits not on doctrine files do not require pause. - **The decision table** — pause-required vs. pause-not-required scenarios in a concise table form (mirroring the pre-strip BlarAI version's structure).
The element name in devplatform is `` (matching the pre-strip BlarAI XML element name — devplatform inherits the original element identity since the content moved here). BlarAI's `` is intentionally a distinct element name to avoid grep collision.
### Directive C — Row #37 — Vikunja envelope split (LA-approved Option A) **Source**: LA comment #521 row #37 + EA-2 prompt §la_arbitrated_dispositions row #37. **Authorship rule**: devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md authors `` + `` envelopes (pure fleet operating model — what SDO does in Phase 1a/1b/2; what EA does in comprehension/execution/completion). The canonical label-id table stays in BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions. devplatform's `` envelope **cross-references** BlarAI's label table via:
```xml Canonical Vikunja label IDs live in BlarAI doctrine. See: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions. This devplatform doctrine does NOT duplicate numeric label IDs to avoid drift. ```
devplatform's `` envelope structure: - `` - `` — 2-3 sentence orientation for fleet agents. - `` — single pointer line as above. - `` — full body: what SDO writes to Vikunja during Phase 1a (comprehension review), Phase 1b (completion review), Phase 2 (EA prompt authoring), Phase 3+ (escalation). Cite gate-label transitions verbatim (`Gate:Pending-SDO` id 9, `Gate:Approved` id 12, etc. — IDs cross-referenced via the label_reference_pointer). - `` — full body: what EA writes during comprehension (gate post + label `Gate:Pending-SDO`), execution (status-only updates), completion (completion comment + label `Gate:Pending-SDO`). - `` — full body: what Co-Lead writes during Phase 2 merge-gate review (trusted_scope auto-merge OR ESCALATE → `Gate:Pending-Human` for la_merge_approve.ps1 fallback), SCR authoring at sprint close. - ``
Do NOT duplicate the 19-tool MCP list — that stays in BlarAI/CLAUDE.md. devplatform may reference the tool list via a pointer of the form: `*See also: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md §Available MCP Tools.*`
### Directive D — Row #27 — `` MIRROR-both **Source**: LA comment #521 row #27. **Authorship rule**: devplatform's `` block is the LA framing with devplatform-context phrasing. Content: - **Role**: Lead Architect, non-developer. - **Profile**: vibe-coder — drives the fleet via natural language, does not write code directly. - **Environment**: Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200, PowerShell 7+, Hyper-V Gen 2. - **Workflow**: LA reviews SDV at sprint start, monitors gate-label transitions during execution, reads SCR + SWAGR at close. - **Communication preference**: concise, no commendations (per `feedback_no_commendations`), no emojis unless requested, spell out acronyms on first use (per `feedback_spell_out_acronyms`).
Mature-not-minimal floor: a cold-read dev-side agent must understand the LA's role and preferences without cross-repo lookup. BlarAI's `` block is the runtime-context mirror; devplatform's is the fleet-context mirror.
### Directive E — Row #12 — devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint doctrine ownership **Source**: LA comment #521 row #12. **Authorship rule**: devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint authors the doctrine that describes how the fleet sprint lifecycle works: - How `docs/sprints/ACTIVE_SPRINT.md` is read (the file stays in BlarAI per SDV §5.3; devplatform describes how to find/use it). - How `docs/active_tasks.yaml` (in BlarAI) is the roster source of truth. - The DEC-15 sprint lifecycle: SDV → Phase 1a/1b/2/3 EA cycle → SCR at sprint close → SWAGR independent audit. - The derived paths table (SDV path / SCR path / SWAGR path / EA reports directory). - Which agents read the SDV (Co-Lead, SDO, EA Code, Sprint Auditor) and what each reads for. - What the pattern does NOT do (e.g., does NOT create cross-sprint dependencies without explicit CAR; does NOT amend ADRs in-sprint). - References to template files in `docs/sprints/_templates/`.
BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L91 has the `*See also: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint.*` pointer — the header text MUST be exactly `## Current-Active-Sprint` (or `### Current-Active-Sprint` — case-sensitive and hyphen-style) for grep-able resolution.
### Additional required §sections in devplatform/CLAUDE.md (from BlarAI EA-2 post-merge pointers)
- **§Vikunja-Bridge** — how Vikunja MCP wiring works at the fleet level. Describe the three install sites (Claude Desktop config `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`, project-scoped `.mcp.json` at BlarAI repo root, VS Code Copilot `.vscode/mcp.json`); how the Vikunja server is started (referenced from BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Server); how the bridge serves SDO/Co-Lead/EA-Code (sandbox agents read state.json/inbox.json/processed.json under `tools/autonomy_budget/` and the Vikunja MCP). BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L68 pointer expects this §section.
- **§Agent-Operating-Model** — Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3 fleet agent hierarchy: - Tier 1: Co-Lead Architect — Phase 2 merge gate, SCR authoring, peer review across SDO ↔ EA Code. - Tier 2: SDO (Strategic Development Orchestrator) — Phase 1a comprehension review, Phase 1b completion review, Phase 2 EA prompt authoring. - Tier 3: EA Code — milestone execution under comprehension → execution → completion gates. - DEC-12 peer-review lattice: which agent reviews which, gate transitions via Vikunja gate-labels. - Gate-label semantics (cross-reference BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions for the numeric IDs; semantic meaning here): - `Gate:Pending-SDO` — agent has handed off; SDO reviews next. - `Gate:Pending-CoLead` — SDO approved; Co-Lead reviews next. - `Gate:Pending-Human` — escalation; LA reviews next. - `Gate:Approved` — passed. - `Gate:Rejected` — failed; returned for rework. - `Gate:Escalation` — Co-Lead or SDO has escalated; LA decides.
- **§Fleet-Pause-SOP** — pointer to the canonical `` element in devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md, with a 1-2 line narrative noting that the SOP is operational dev-side norm (NOT BlarAI runtime constraint) and the LA-coordination expectation. The full SOP body lives in the XML doctrine; CLAUDE.md §Fleet-Pause-SOP is a narrative landing page.

work_items.wi:
**Author devplatform/CLAUDE.md (≥ 100 lines, target 200-300).**
Overwrite the existing 4-line placeholder. The file must contain (at minimum) these §sections in this order:
- **Title** — `# DevPlatform — Project Instructions for Claude Desktop` (or similar — author discretion on exact header text, but the file is the fleet-side counterpart to BlarAI/CLAUDE.md). - **§Repo Identity** — devplatform is the dev-platform / fleet infrastructure repo, NOT a product runtime. It hosts the autonomy budget, wake templates, scheduled-task triggers, fleet observability tooling. BlarAI is the product runtime — devplatform is the harness. - **§Vikunja-Bridge** — per la_arbitrated_directives "Additional required §sections" above. ≥ 15 lines. - **§Current-Active-Sprint** — per la_arbitrated_directives Directive E above. ≥ 25 lines (the doctrine substance). - **§Agent-Operating-Model** — per la_arbitrated_directives "Additional required §sections" above. ≥ 30 lines (Tier 1/2/3 + DEC-12 lattice + gate-label semantics). - **§Fleet-Pause-SOP** — narrative landing page with pointer to the XML element in devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md. ≥ 5 lines. - **§DEC References** — short subsection enumerating DEC-11 (autonomy budget), DEC-12 (peer-review lattice), DEC-13 (Fleet Reports queue), DEC-14.5 (trusted_scope merge model), DEC-15 (sprint lifecycle). Each DEC: 1-line description, pointer to its source doc (if any) in BlarAI's `docs/` tree or in devplatform itself. ≥ 10 lines. - **§Wake Templates** — short subsection enumerating the 4 wake-template files (SDO/Co-Lead/EA-Code/Sprint-Auditor) at `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\scheduled-tasks\templates\` (or wherever they live — verify path at EA-start). Do NOT mirror their contents; cite that the templates themselves are authoritative. ≥ 5 lines. - **§Cross-References to BlarAI** — explicit pointers to BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §sections for content that stays in BlarAI (UCs, ADRs, hardware, security, coding standards, Vikunja Conventions for label IDs). Use the SDV §5.3 mandated italicized cross-reference style: `*See also: \`\` §
*`. ≥ 8 cross-references minimum.
**Header-text constraint**: §Vikunja-Bridge, §Current-Active-Sprint, §Agent-Operating-Model, §Fleet-Pause-SOP must use those EXACT names (hyphenated, case-sensitive) so BlarAI's `*See also:*` pointers resolve via grep. Any other §section in devplatform/CLAUDE.md may use author discretion.
**Verification mid-WI**: after authoring, grep BlarAI's post-EA-2 doctrine for `devplatform\\CLAUDE\.md` pointers and confirm each pointer's `§` exists in your authored devplatform/CLAUDE.md.

**Author devplatform/AGENTS.md (≥ 100 lines, target 120-180).**
Overwrite the existing 2-line placeholder. Content per la_arbitrated_directives Directive A above:
- **Title** — `# AGENTS.md — devplatform repo pointer (fleet infrastructure)`. - **Opening paragraph** — devplatform is the dev-platform / fleet-infrastructure repo; it hosts the autonomy_budget, wake templates, scheduled tasks, fleet observability. NOT a runtime product. - **Dev/target framing** — Claude / Codex / Copilot are dev-side agents. They target TWO repos: BlarAI (product runtime) and devplatform (this repo, fleet infrastructure). When you read this file, you are operating ON devplatform — likely doing fleet-tooling work, wake-template adjustments, autonomy_budget code, or doctrine maintenance. - **Authoritative-reference pointers** — `devplatform/CLAUDE.md` for fleet operating model; `devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md` for Copilot/dev-side-agent operating instructions; BlarAI's AGENTS.md at `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\AGENTS.md` as the sibling target-repo entry. - **Fleet-coordination expectations** — per-role 2-3 sentences: - **SDO** (Strategic Development Orchestrator) — autonomous; wakes on cron; Phase 1a comprehension review, Phase 1b completion review, Phase 2 EA prompt authoring. - **Co-Lead Architect** — autonomous; wakes on cron and on demand; Phase 2 merge-gate review, SCR authoring at sprint close, peer-review across SDO ↔ EA Code. - **EA Code** — autonomous; wakes on trigger files in `tools/scheduled-tasks/triggers/`; milestone execution under comprehension → execution → completion gates; writes to BlarAI worktrees + devplatform direct-to-main per work item. - **Sprint Auditor** — autonomous; wakes post-SCR; independent SWAGR production with pytest regression baseline. - **BlarAI as ONE of two target repos** — explicitly: `BlarAI is a Qwen3-based locally-run product runtime at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI. Claude is NEVER part of BlarAI's runtime; BlarAI's own UC-005 Code Agent (future Qwen3 agent) is the runtime-side coding assistant. When dev-side agents touch BlarAI, they treat BlarAI/CLAUDE.md as authoritative for runtime architecture, ADRs, Use Cases, security mandates.` - **Fleet-pause SOP pointer** — single-line italicized pointer to `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop` and a 1-sentence note that fleet-pause is required before substantive multi-commit dev work. - **Footer** — "This file is intentionally substantive (≥ 100 lines) to satisfy SDV §5.3 mature-not-minimal floor. It is read by Codex / Cowork sandbox agents and Copilot sessions that may have no preloaded context."
**Length floor verification**: `(Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\AGENTS.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines` ≥ 100.

**Author devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md (≥ 100 lines, target 250-400).**
Overwrite the existing 2-line placeholder. The file is XML (mirroring BlarAI's format). Required envelope structure:
```xml
...
...
...
... ... ... ...
direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5 convention Windows 11 Pro, PowerShell 7+, Hyper-V Gen 2 If validation scripts fail, DO NOT delete the branch. Preserve it for audit. ...
... Canonical Vikunja label IDs live in BlarAI doctrine. See: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions. This devplatform doctrine does NOT duplicate numeric label IDs to avoid drift. ... ... ...
EXECUTE FLEET DIRECTIVES AUTONOMOUSLY
```
**`` body (Directive B — authoritative)**: contains the canonical SOP. Author the full block here. Required children inside ``: - `` — multi-commit, branch-checkout, merge, stash. List as `` children. - `` — verbatim PowerShell command using EA-3's chosen portability technique (post-fix). Example for option (c) standalone CLI: ```powershell python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py pause "" --updated-by cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI ; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- " ``` - `` — symmetric form using `resume_fleet` (NOT `unpause_fleet` — API-name gotcha): ```powershell python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py resume --updated-by cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI ; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- " ``` - `` — how to confirm pause/resume landed (state.json inspection per Directive B above). - `` — fleet-pause is LA-coordinated; ambiguous instructions → read back and ask. Per feedback memory. - `` — single-commit non-doctrine edits do NOT require pause. - `` — pause-required vs. pause-not-required scenarios, table form.
**XML well-formedness (L-20)**: after authoring, verify by: ```powershell python -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md')" ``` Must exit 0. Any parse error → fix XML, retest.
**Length floor**: ≥ 100 lines; target 250-400 lines given the SOP body density alone is ~30-50 lines.

**SOP import portability fix (SDV §4 success criterion #4).**
Resolve `from tools.autonomy_budget import state` import portability so that pause + resume invocations succeed from at least 3 working directories: `C:\`, `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI`, `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform`.
**Implementation choice**: one of the SDV §9.2 #3 catalog options (declared in comprehension gate item #13). Default per SDV is **option (c) — standalone CLI script** `tools/autonomy_budget/cli.py`.
**Option (c) implementation sketch** (if chosen — adjust for other options as needed):
Create `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py`:
```python """Standalone CLI for autonomy_budget pause/resume.
Designed to be invoked by absolute path from any working directory:
python C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform\\tools\\autonomy_budget\\cli.py pause "" --updated-by python C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform\\tools\\autonomy_budget\\cli.py resume --updated-by
The script's containing directory (`tools/autonomy_budget/`) is automatically prepended to `sys.path` by Python when invoked by absolute path, so `from tools.autonomy_budget import state` resolves correctly via a `sys.path` augmentation step below. """ from __future__ import annotations
import argparse import sys from pathlib import Path
# Ensure devplatform repo root is on sys.path so `tools.autonomy_budget` is importable # regardless of the cwd from which this script is invoked. _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] # tools/autonomy_budget/cli.py → devplatform/ if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path: sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
from tools.autonomy_budget import state # noqa: E402
_DEFAULT_STATE_PATH = str(_REPO_ROOT / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "state.json")
def _cmd_pause(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int: state.pause_fleet(args.reason, updated_by=args.updated_by, path=args.state_path) print(f"fleet paused: reason={args.reason!r} updated_by={args.updated_by!r}") return 0
def _cmd_resume(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int: state.resume_fleet(updated_by=args.updated_by, path=args.state_path) print(f"fleet resumed: updated_by={args.updated_by!r}") return 0
def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="autonomy_budget.cli") sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
p_pause = sub.add_parser("pause", help="Pause the fleet") p_pause.add_argument("reason") p_pause.add_argument("--updated-by", required=True) p_pause.add_argument("--state-path", default=_DEFAULT_STATE_PATH) p_pause.set_defaults(func=_cmd_pause)
p_resume = sub.add_parser("resume", help="Resume the fleet") p_resume.add_argument("--updated-by", required=True) p_resume.add_argument("--state-path", default=_DEFAULT_STATE_PATH) p_resume.set_defaults(func=_cmd_resume)
args = parser.parse_args(argv) return args.func(args)
if __name__ == "__main__": raise SystemExit(main()) ```
The actual implementation may differ — the above is a sketch. EA Code may adapt to match `state.py`'s existing API signature exactly. Verify by reading `state.py` first.
**Verification matrix (SDV §4 success criterion #4)**: run pause AND resume invocations from THREE working directories, capture stdout, and confirm NO `ModuleNotFoundError`:
```powershell # From C:\ cd C:\ python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py pause "EA-3 portability verification" --updated-by ea_code python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py resume --updated-by ea_code
# From C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py pause "EA-3 portability verification" --updated-by ea_code python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py resume --updated-by ea_code
# From C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py pause "EA-3 portability verification" --updated-by ea_code python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py resume --updated-by ea_code ```
Expected outputs: 3 × pause stdout, 3 × resume stdout, zero `ModuleNotFoundError`. Capture all 6 outputs verbatim into the completion report's verification section.
**NOTE on the verification's state-mutation impact**: each pause + resume pair leaves state.json in the post-resume state. The verification's 3 pause/resume cycles all return to resumed state at the end of each cycle, so the final state after WI-4 verification is `resumed`. If the fleet was paused during the EA (per pre-flight pause), the WI-4 verification runs AFTER the WI-4 fix is committed and AFTER the regular pre-flight pause; you may need to re-pause after WI-4 verification before continuing to subsequent WIs, OR sequence WI-4 verification immediately before the pre-exit resume. Document the chosen sequence in the completion report.

**devplatform direct-to-main commit.**
Commit all four content artifacts (3 doctrine files + 1+ portability-fix file) in a single direct-to-main commit on devplatform. Commit message per `commit_template` below. Commit body cross-references the BlarAI EA-2 merge commit `1b1614e` verbatim.
```powershell cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git add CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md .github\copilot-instructions.md tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py # If the chosen portability fix touches additional files, add them explicitly by name. git status # confirm only the four+ intended paths are staged git commit -m "[sprint:10][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-3 devplatform doctrine authorship + SOP portability fix" -m "" git log --oneline -1 # capture commit hash for completion report ```
Confirm no other devplatform paths got swept into the commit via `git status` post-commit (should be clean modulo the pre-existing dirty working tree items: state.json, the three wake-template XMLs, fleet_observability flags — those are operational artifacts NOT EA-3 changes). If any of those are staged accidentally, unstage and recommit.

**BlarAI ledger entry (Q1-1 per docs/ledger/README.md).**
Path: `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\20260511_180000_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md`. If the EA-start UTC stamp differs materially from `20260511_180000`, adjust the filename to actual second-resolution UTC; otherwise the pre-computed stamp is acceptable.
Required frontmatter (verbatim):
```yaml --- ledger_id: 20260511_180000_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship date: 2026-05-11 sprint_id: 10 entry_type: EA predecessor: 20260511_222928_sprint10_ea2_blarai-strip branch: direct-to-main (devplatform) merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE --- ```
Verify predecessor by `ls C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\` at EA start — the most recent EA-2 entry is `20260511_222928_sprint10_ea2_blarai-strip.md`. If your verification disagrees, use the actual filename stem.
Body sections (mature-not-minimal — substantive):
- **Summary** (1-2 paragraphs) — what was authored in devplatform; what portability technique was chosen; cross-references between BlarAI (post-EA-2) and devplatform (post-EA-3) now resolve cleanly. - **Deliverables** — 4 files: 3 devplatform doctrine files (with line counts before/after) + 1+ portability fix file (with cohesive description). Add cross-repo: 1 BlarAI ledger entry (this file) + 1 BlarAI sprint completion report. - **Files Changed** — cross-repo enumeration: - BlarAI: ledger entry + sprint completion report - devplatform: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, tools/autonomy_budget/cli.py (or whichever portability artifact) - **Quality Gate** — STRUCTURE-LINT pass, XML well-formedness pass (paste verbatim parse-success confirmation), LA-arbitrated-directives conformance pass (5 directives by name), cross-reference resolution pass (BlarAI pointers L68/L91/post-Active-State + XML pointers L134-136/L165 all resolve), verification matrix outputs (6 invocations × no ModuleNotFoundError), commit hash. - **Verification Matrix** (SDV §4 #4) — table form: 3 working directories × 2 commands (pause/resume) × verbatim stdout. Confirm zero ModuleNotFoundError across all 6 invocations. - **Cross-Reference Resolution Audit** — confirm each post-EA-2 BlarAI pointer resolves to a §section or XML element in EA-3's devplatform doctrine. List each pointer with its resolution status (RESOLVED / DANGLING — if any DANGLING, fix before commit). - **Length-count summary** — devplatform three-file combined line count (target ≥ 300 floor, ≥ 400 mature target). BlarAI doctrine file line counts unchanged from post-EA-2 baseline.

**Sprint completion report on BlarAI worktree.**
Path: `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_10\reports\_ea_code_completion_v1.md` where `` is the UTC `YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS` captured at EA close.
Body sections (consistent with prior EA completion reports under `docs/sprints/sprint_10/reports/`):
- **Header** — sprint_id, ea_number, agent, phase, parent_head_blarai (`1b1614e`), parent_head_devplatform (live HEAD at EA-3 start, captured), commit hashes (BlarAI side for ledger+report; devplatform side for content). - **Summary** — 2-3 paragraphs. - **Work item disposition** — per-WI status (WI-1 through WI-7), each ACHIEVED / DEVIATION-DOCUMENTED / BLOCKED. No WI should be BLOCKED at completion — if any are, do not call this a completion; call it a partial. - **Acceptance criteria checklist** — recite the 8 acceptance criteria from §acceptance_criteria below, each with PASS / FAIL and evidence. - **Quality gate outputs** — STRUCTURE-LINT, XML well-formedness command + output, LA-directives conformance, cross-reference resolution audit, verification matrix outputs. - **Devplatform commit** — hash + message + cross-repo body reference. - **BlarAI ledger entry** — path + frontmatter recap. - **Verification matrix** — 6 invocations × verbatim stdout. - **Cross-reference resolution audit** — pointer-by-pointer status. - **Negative-constraints compliance** — N-1 through N-N from §negative_constraints below, each confirmed. - **Handoff** — next fleet step (SDO Phase 1b review pickup via Vikunja gate-label transition + trigger file).
Then commit the ledger entry + sprint report on BlarAI main (or on a trivial branch and merge via Co-Lead trusted_scope — author discretion since the BlarAI-side changes are metadata-only and well under the 500-LOC threshold). Use commit message:
``` [sprint:10][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-3 sprint completion report + ledger entry ```
with body referencing the devplatform commit hash.

file.type: overwrite (placeholder → ≥100 line XML doctrine with full fleet_pause_sop body)

commit.message_template: [sprint:10][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-3 devplatform doctrine authorship + SOP portability fix
[sprint:10][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-3 sprint completion report + ledger entry

acceptance_criteria.criterion: `devplatform/CLAUDE.md` exists, ≥ 100 lines, contains §sections named EXACTLY: `Current-Active-Sprint`, `Vikunja-Bridge`, `Agent-Operating-Model`, `Fleet-Pause-SOP` (case-sensitive, hyphenated). File is self-readable without preloaded BlarAI context.

`devplatform/AGENTS.md` exists, ≥ 100 lines, contains dev/target framing per la_arbitrated_directives Directive A, points to `devplatform/CLAUDE.md` and `devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md`, explicitly names BlarAI as one of two target repos with `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI` absolute path.

`devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md` exists, ≥ 100 lines, contains the `` element (full body per Directive B), the `` + `` + `` envelopes (Directive C), the `` block (Directive D), the `` cross-referencing BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions (Directive C). Parses cleanly via `python -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse(r'...')"` (exit 0).

SOP portability fix in place; pause + resume invocations succeed from 3 working directories (`C:\`, `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI`, `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform`) with zero `ModuleNotFoundError`. All 6 invocation stdouts captured verbatim in the completion report's Verification Matrix section.

Ledger entry written at `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md` with frontmatter complete (ledger_id, date, sprint_id=10, entry_type=EA, predecessor=EA-2 ledger stem, branch=direct-to-main (devplatform), merge_commit=null, disposition=COMPLETE).

BlarAI post-EA-2 cross-reference pointers resolve cleanly to §sections / XML elements EA-3 authored — no dangling references. Specifically: - BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L68 `*See also: ...§Vikunja-Bridge.*` → devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja-Bridge - BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L91 `*See also: ...§Current-Active-Sprint.*` → devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint - BlarAI/CLAUDE.md `*See also: ...§Agent-Operating-Model and §Fleet-Pause-SOP.*` → devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Agent-Operating-Model + §Fleet-Pause-SOP - BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md L134-136 `` → devplatform `` - BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md L165 `` → devplatform `` envelope Audit listed in completion report with PASS/FAIL per pointer.

devplatform commit body cross-references BlarAI EA-2 merge commit `1b1614e` verbatim (or whatever commit hash captures EA-2's actual merge — verify by `git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" log --oneline -10 main` at EA start; the la_merge_approve commit landing EA-2 is the reference).
EA-3 sprint completion report at `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_10\reports\_ea_code_completion_v1.md` committed on BlarAI main with completion phase tag.

negative_constraints.constraint: NO changes to BlarAI doctrine files (CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md). Those were EA-2's scope and are frozen for the remainder of Sprint 10. (SDV §5.1 #3 implies EA-2 ↔ EA-3 boundary.)

NO changes to ADRs (`docs/adrs/ADR-*.md`), governance docs (`docs/governance/*.md`), or runbooks (`docs/runbooks/*.md`). If EA-3 surfaces a stale reference, STOP and document in the completion report. Do not freelance. (SDV §5.2 #3-#4.)

NO changes to test code (`tests/`, `services/*/tests/`, `launcher/tests/`, `shared/tests/`) or production code (`services/`, `shared/`, `launcher/`). Sprint 10 is doctrine-only + one focused portability fix in `tools/autonomy_budget/`. (SDV §5.2 #1.)
NO repo-rename, directory-restructure, or path-layout changes. (SDV §5.2 #9, §13 #4.)
NO duplication of the canonical Vikunja label-id table. devplatform doctrine cross-references BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions; does NOT enumerate numeric IDs. (Directive C.)

NO BlarAI feature branch for EA-3 work. EA-3 has no BlarAI source-doctrine writes; the ledger + report commit directly to BlarAI main. devplatform commits direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5 convention. (Working-directory exception §3.)
NO touching `docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md` — FROZEN at Entry 52. New entries land in `docs/ledger/` per-file (Q1-1 format). (BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Active State.)

NO Stage 6.7.5 retro-fix tickets created by EA-3. Separate hardening followups go through SDO Stage 6.7.5 backlog. If EA-3 finds a defect deserving a retro-fix ticket, record it as a "Finding for Stage 6.7.5 triage" in the completion report; do not create the Vikunja ticket from EA-3. (Per `feedback_doc_cleanup_non_optional` — existence is non-optional; LA may triage at Stage 6 execution; ticket creation is NOT EA-3 scope.)
NO push to either remote. Local commits only. (Working-directory exception §5.)

NO modification of pre-existing dirty-working-tree items on devplatform (state.json, the three wake-template XMLs, fleet_observability flags). Those are operational artifacts, not EA-3 deliverables. Stage ONLY the four+ paths intended by EA-3. Use explicit `git add ` per file; never `git add -A`. (Working-directory exception §4 + safety hygiene.)

NO XML malformedness. After authoring devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md, MUST parse cleanly via `python -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse(r'...')"` (exit 0). Test verbatim before commit. (L-20, mirroring EA-2 N-9.)

NO sacrificing mature-not-minimal coherence to hit the ≥ 100 line floor with padding. If a file approaches 100 lines only with bullet-point lists of cross-references, expand with concrete operational guidance from the matrix MOVE-devplatform content and SDV §5.3 (a)-(i) enumeration. The floor is a floor; the target is content density. (L-22 mature-not-minimal verbatim.)

NO alteration of the verbatim LA-arbitrated content directives (Directives A-E from §la_arbitrated_directives). Where the LA supplied verbatim framing or specific element names (``, ``, ``, ``, §Current-Active-Sprint header), use those EXACTLY. Freelance phrasing changes to the LA-arbitrated portions are an automatic ESCALATE. (Mirroring EA-2 N-8.)
NO new Vikunja tickets created by EA-3 for findings outside the working set. (Mirroring EA-2 N-11; matrix §4 Findings flow through SCR / SWAGR triage.)

NO touching `tools/vikunja_mcp/`, `tools/vikunja/`, services/, shared/, launcher/, phase2_gates/, or any other repo path not explicitly listed in the deliverable. The single allowed code touch is the portability fix under `tools/autonomy_budget/`. (SDV §5.2 #6, §5.2 #8.)

NO running pytest as part of EA-3 work. Sprint 10 is doctrine-only with one focused code touch; the portability fix has no test target (it's an entry-point shim). If the portability technique chosen DOES warrant a unit test (e.g., import-path resolution test), declare in comprehension gate and add the test file to the working set — but the default is no pytest run. (Mirroring EA-2 regression-only run; EA-3's verification is the 3-working-directory invocation matrix, not pytest.)

ea_prompt.cross_repo_ordering:
Per L-19 (b), recite this verbatim in your comprehension and your completion report:
"EA-2 landed on BlarAI main first at commit `1b1614e` (LA-merge-approve via la_merge_approve.ps1, DEC-14.5). EA-3 commits to devplatform main second via direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5 convention. My devplatform commit body explicitly cross-references the BlarAI EA-2 merge commit `1b1614e`. I do NOT push to either remote. I do NOT touch BlarAI doctrine files — they were EA-2's scope and are now frozen for the remainder of Sprint 10."
**Cross-repo commit-body content for EA-3** (devplatform side, verbatim within the commit body):
``` BlarAI companion commit: 1b1614e (Sprint 10 EA-2 BlarAI doctrine strip + Active State refresh + AGENTS.md pointer via la_merge_approve, DEC-14.5).
This commit authors devplatform/CLAUDE.md, devplatform/AGENTS.md, and devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md from scratch per Sprint 10 EA-1's classification matrix (MOVE-devplatform rows + MIRROR-both rows) and the LA-arbitrated content directives in Vikunja task #369 comment #521. Also resolves the tools.autonomy_budget import portability bug (SDV §4 success criterion #4) via a standalone CLI script invocable by absolute path from any working directory.
Verification matrix: pause + resume invocations succeed from C:\, C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI, C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform with zero ModuleNotFoundError. See sprint completion report at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_10\reports\_ea_code_completion_v1.md for full verification outputs.
Closes Sprint 10's three-EA chain. SCR authoring follows by Co-Lead. ```
(Adjust "standalone CLI script" wording if EA-3 chose a different portability-fix option.)

quality_gate.step: All three devplatform doctrine files have valid markdown / XML structure. No orphan headers, no XML mis-nesting, no unmatched code-fence triple-backticks. devplatform/CLAUDE.md has §sections matching the BlarAI cross-reference pointer text EXACTLY (case + hyphenation).

```powershell python -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md')" ``` Must exit 0 with no error output. Any parse error → STOP, fix XML, retest. (L-20)

```powershell (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\AGENTS.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines ``` Each must return ≥ 100. Any file < 100 lines → STOP, expand with concrete content per L-22 mature-not-minimal carve-out, retest.

The five LA-arbitrated directives are applied: (A) devplatform/AGENTS.md dev/target framing — ≥ 100 lines, dual-target framing, BlarAI as one of two repos. (B) devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md owns full `` element with all required children (trigger_conditions, pause_command, resume_command, verification_step, la_coordination_note, trivial_edit_exception, decision_table). (C) devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md `` envelope contains `` + `` + `` envelopes + `` cross-referencing BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions — NO numeric label IDs duplicated. (D) devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md `` block present with LA framing + devplatform-context phrasing. (E) devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint authored with full doctrine substance (≥ 25 lines).

Each BlarAI post-EA-2 cross-reference pointer resolves to a §section or XML element EA-3 authored: - BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L68 → devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja-Bridge (grep -nE '^#+ +Vikunja-Bridge' devplatform/CLAUDE.md returns ≥ 1 match) - BlarAI/CLAUDE.md L91 → devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint (grep match) - BlarAI/CLAUDE.md post-Active-State `§Agent-Operating-Model and §Fleet-Pause-SOP` → both §sections present - BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md L134-136 `` → devplatform `` (grep -nE ']' devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md returns ≥ 1 match) - BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md L165 `` → devplatform `` envelope present List each pointer with RESOLVED / DANGLING in the completion report. Any DANGLING → STOP, fix, retest.
Pause + resume invocations from 3 working directories (SDV §4 #4). Capture all 6 stdouts verbatim. Confirm zero `ModuleNotFoundError`. (Per WI-4 verification section.)

```powershell git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" diff 1a4713d..HEAD --name-only ``` Must output EXACTLY (sorted, modulo portability-fix file paths): ``` .github/copilot-instructions.md AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md tools/autonomy_budget/cli.py (or chosen alternative) ``` Any other path → ORACLE violation; STOP and report. (Note: pre-existing dirty working tree items in devplatform — state.json mutations, wake-template XML edits, fleet_observability flags — are NOT included in EA-3's commit per N-10; the diff is HEAD-to-HEAD-1 not vs. working tree.)

```powershell git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" diff 1b1614e..HEAD --name-only ``` Must output EXACTLY (sorted): ``` docs/ledger/20260511_180000_sprint10_ea3_devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md docs/sprints/sprint_10/reports/_ea_code_completion_v1.md ``` Any other path → ORACLE violation; STOP and report.

```powershell git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" log -1 --format=%B ``` Body must contain the literal string `1b1614e` (the BlarAI EA-2 merge commit hash) at least once. Any missing cross-reference → STOP, amend commit, retest.

ea_prompt.verification:
Verbatim terminal commands for the LA to verify EA-3 completion:
```powershell # devplatform commit landed git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" log --oneline -1 git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform" diff 1a4713d..HEAD --name-only
# Line-count floor (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\AGENTS.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines
# XML well-formedness python -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse(r'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md')"
# Cross-reference resolution spot-check Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md -Pattern '^#+ +(Vikunja-Bridge|Current-Active-Sprint|Agent-Operating-Model|Fleet-Pause-SOP)$' Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.github\copilot-instructions.md -Pattern '<(fleet_pause_sop|vikunja_task_tracking|sdo_responsibilities|ea_responsibilities|user_identity)[ >]'
# Portability fix verification (sample one directory; completion report has all 3) cd C:\ python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py pause "LA-verify" --updated-by la python C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\cli.py resume --updated-by la
# BlarAI side metadata commits git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" log --oneline -2 git -C "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI" diff 1b1614e..HEAD --name-only ```
Expected: - devplatform last-commit subject matches commit_template; body contains `1b1614e`. - Each devplatform doctrine file ≥ 100 lines. - XML parse exits 0. - All 4 §section headers and 5 XML elements present. - Portability fix: no ModuleNotFoundError from C:\. - BlarAI: 2 new files (ledger + report); no other paths.
ea_prompt.commit_template:
[sprint:10][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-3 devplatform doctrine authorship + SOP portability fix
Body content: Sprint 10 EA-3. Authored devplatform/CLAUDE.md, devplatform/AGENTS.md, and devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md from scratch per Sprint 10 EA-1's classification matrix (MOVE-devplatform rows + MIRROR-both rows) and the LA-arbitrated content directives in Vikunja task #369 comment #521.
Closes Sprint 10's three-EA chain (EA-1 classification matrix → EA-2 BlarAI strip → EA-3 devplatform authorship). SCR authoring follows by Co-Lead.
Key content directives encoded: - devplatform/AGENTS.md mirrors the dev/target boundary framing authored by EA-2 in BlarAI/AGENTS.md; ≥ 100 lines (mature-not- minimal floor per SDV §5.3). - devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md owns the full element (BlarAI's resolves here). - devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md envelope contains + + ; cross-references BlarAI/CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions for the canonical label-id table (does NOT duplicate numeric label IDs). - devplatform/CLAUDE.md §Current-Active-Sprint authors the fleet sprint-lifecycle doctrine; describes how BlarAI's docs/sprints/ACTIVE_SPRINT.md is read (file stays in BlarAI).
Also resolves `from tools.autonomy_budget import state` import portability bug (SDV §4 success criterion #4) via [chosen technique]. Verified pause + resume succeed from C:\, C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI, and C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform with zero ModuleNotFoundError. Full verification matrix in EA-3 completion report.
BlarAI companion commit: 1b1614e (Sprint 10 EA-2 BlarAI doctrine strip + Active State refresh + AGENTS.md pointer via la_merge_approve, DEC-14.5).
Sprint completion report: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_10\ reports\_ea_code_completion_v1.md. Ledger entry: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\_sprint10_ea3_ devplatform-doctrine-authorship.md.
ea_prompt.mature_not_minimal:
Verbatim from L-22:
"Mature not minimal: each post-split file is a coherent operational reference in its own right, not a thin pointer-with-a-couple-of-sentences. devplatform files: ≥ 100 lines floor. BlarAI files: target ≥ 30% combined-line-count reduction (soft 50%). If hitting the reduction floor would leave dangling references or break a coherent runtime narrative, prefer coherence and explicitly cite which sections you kept verbatim despite hitting the floor in your completion report. The LA will not penalize a coherent file that fell short of the reduction target; the LA will penalize an incoherent file that hit it."
EA-3-specific application: each of the three devplatform doctrine files MUST hit the ≥ 100 line floor (SDV §4 #2 verification is explicit). The mature target is content density, not padding. devplatform/CLAUDE.md mature target 200-300 lines; devplatform/AGENTS.md mature target 120-180 lines; devplatform/.github/copilot-instructions.md mature target 250-400 lines (the SOP body alone is ~30-50 lines; the rule envelope adds another ~50-80; the vikunja_task_tracking envelope adds another ~50-80).
If a file approaches 100 lines with only bullet-point lists of cross-references, expand by drawing on the matrix MOVE-devplatform rows (the substantive content that exists pre-strip in BlarAI's doctrine and MUST be authored fresh in devplatform): the SDO + EA role descriptions (rows #28 + #29), the eight named `` elements that moved (#32f, #32h, #32j-l, #32n-o), the full `` body (#35e), the §Current Active Sprint doctrine (#10-15), the §Agent Operating Model 7-step list (#19), and the §Fleet-Pause SOP decision table (#21). Also draw from the matrix fresh-content rows (#45-54): DEC-11 autonomy budget summary, DEC-12 peer-review lattice description, DEC-13 Fleet Reports queue, DEC-14.5 trusted_scope merge model, wake-template summary, autonomy_budget state.json schema, EA prompt XML format conventions.
ea_prompt.phase_transitions:
Standard EA Code Case C (completion) → SDO Phase 1b review.
- **EA Code Case C (completion)**: EA-3 completes work, runs quality gates, commits per template, posts completion comment to Vikunja task #369 with `Gate:Pending-SDO` label (id 9), writes trigger file for SDO wake, calls `schtasks /run /tn "\BlarAI\Wake SDO"`, exits. - **SDO Phase 1b (completion review)**: SDO wakes, reads completion comment + ledger entry + sprint report, verifies acceptance criteria (8 items), runs cross-reference resolution audit independently, runs verification matrix spot-check (1 of 3 directories), runs XML parse, runs line-count check. Verdict: APPROVED / REJECTED / ESCALATE. - **SDO Phase 1b APPROVED → NO Co-Lead Phase 2 merge needed**: EA-3 has NO BlarAI feature branch to merge (devplatform is direct-to-main; BlarAI side is metadata-only direct commits). SDO Phase 1b APPROVED transitions directly to Co-Lead authoring the SCR. - **Co-Lead Phase 3 (SCR authoring)**: Co-Lead reads SDV, all 3 EA completion reports, all 3 ledger entries, verifies Stage 6 v1 items 6.1/6.2/6.3/6.6 are recorded CLOSED with merge commits, authors `docs/sprints/sprint_10/strategic_completion_report.md`. - **Sprint Auditor (SWAGR independent audit)**: Sprint Auditor wakes post-SCR, runs SWAGR independent audit, writes `docs/sprints/sprint_10/Strategic_Work_Analysis_and_Gap_Report_Sprint_10_.md`. - **Sprint 10 closed**: LA reads SCR + SWAGR, signs off, fleet may re-pause for cf-1 prep at LA's discretion.
ea_prompt.handoff_protocol:
On completion: 1. Run all 9 quality_gate steps in order. If any step fails, STOP and document in completion comment (do not commit until all pass). 2. Commit on devplatform main per commit_template (devplatform side). Verify commit body contains `1b1614e` literal. 3. Run WI-4 verification matrix (3 working dirs × 2 commands = 6 invocations); capture all stdouts. 4. Write ledger entry (WI-6) and sprint completion report (WI-7) on BlarAI worktree. 5. Commit ledger + report on BlarAI main (or trivial branch + Co-Lead merge — author discretion since changes are metadata-only). 6. Post `[agent:ea_code][phase:completion]` comment to Vikunja task #369 with: - devplatform commit hash + verbatim subject line - BlarAI commit hash(es) for ledger + report - Three devplatform file line counts (target ≥ 100 each) - XML well-formedness pass confirmation - LA-directives conformance: confirm 5 directives applied (A-E) - Cross-reference resolution audit: 5 BlarAI pointers all RESOLVED - Verification matrix: 3 dirs × 2 cmds = 6 invocations, zero ModuleNotFoundError, all stdouts in report - ORACLE-devplatform output verbatim (3 doctrine files + portability-fix path) - ORACLE-BlarAI output verbatim (2 metadata files) - Resume-fleet confirmation - Any L-22 coherence carve-out citations (sections kept verbatim despite exceeding mature target — unlikely on devplatform side since target is content-density, not minimization) - Any findings outside the working set deferred to Stage 6.7.5 backlog (N-8) 7. Apply label `Gate:Pending-SDO` (id 9) on Vikunja task #369 for Phase 1b review pickup. 8. Resume the fleet per pre_flight (LAST action before exit). 9. Write trigger file for SDO wake: ```powershell New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\scheduled-tasks\triggers | Out-Null Set-Content -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\scheduled-tasks\triggers\sdo.wake -Value "sprint10_ea3_completion" schtasks /run /tn "\BlarAI\Wake SDO" ```
Next fleet step: SDO Phase 1b completion-review. If APPROVED → Co-Lead Phase 3 SCR authoring directly (no Phase 2 merge gate needed since no BlarAI feature branch). After SCR → Sprint Auditor SWAGR → Sprint 10 closed.
